success_surface: Sheet-Level Row Deletion Based on Content Search
effective_behaviors: Two-phase approach: inspect data structure before implementing transformation logic
Use openpyxl for structural modifications (row/column operations) rather than pandas
Case-insensitive string matching when searching for substrings across cells
Reverse-order iteration when deleting multiple rows to preserve index integrity
Conditional execution guards: verify target exists and meets criteria before modifying
Post-operation validation: print remaining content to confirm expected results
evidence: Agent first explored spreadsheet with inspection code before generating final solution
Solution used openpyxl.load_workbook() instead of pandas for row manipulation capability
Search implementation: 'domestic' in cell_value.lower() for case-insensitive matching
Row deletion loop: range(rows_to_delete, 0, -1) ensures indices don't shift during deletion
Guard clause: 'if target_row and target_row > 1:' prevented unnecessary operations
Final verification printed remaining rows confirming row 19 became row 1 after deletion

memory_items.title: Inspect Before Manipulate
memory_items.lesson: Always examine data structure and locate targets before implementing transformations
memory_items.generalization: For any spreadsheet operation requiring conditional logic based on content, perform an inspection pass first to understand schema, locate key cells, and validate assumptions.

memory_items.title: Reverse Iteration for Row Deletions
memory_items.lesson: When deleting multiple consecutive rows, iterate from highest index to lowest
memory_items.generalization: Row removal operations must proceed backwards (descending order) to prevent index misalignment as subsequent rows shift upward after each deletion.

memory_items.title: Robust String Searching
memory_items.lesson: Use case-insensitive matching and handle non-string cell values gracefully
memory_items.generalization: When searching for text patterns in spreadsheet cells, normalize case comparison and verify value types before string operations to avoid exceptions on empty or numeric cells.

memory_items.title: Structural Operations Need openpyxl
memory_items.lesson: pandas cannot delete rows or preserve formula references during structural changes
memory_items.generalization: For tasks involving row/column insertion/deletion, merged cell management, or preserving formulas/formats, use openpyxl exclusively rather than pandas for the modification phase.

memory_items.title: Verify Before Finalizing
memory_items.lesson: Print or log post-operation state to confirm expected outcomes
memory_items.generalization: After any structural modification, validate key metrics (row count, target content position) programmatically before considering the operation complete.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Sheet-Level Structural Modifications
- **Inspect First**: Examine data structure and locate target content before implementing transformations. Understanding the full scope prevents incorrect assumptions.
- **Reverse Row Deletion**: When deleting multiple rows, always iterate from highest to lowest index to maintain correct row alignment after each deletion.
- **openpyxl for Structure**: Use openpyxl for row/column insertion and deletion operations; pandas is not suitable for structural changes that preserve formulas and formatting.
- **Defensive Content Search**: Normalize case (lower()/upper()) and check cell value types before string operations to handle mixed-type columns safely.
- **Conditional Guards**: Verify target conditions are met (found target, valid range) before executing bulk modifications to prevent unintended data loss.
- **Post-Operation Validation**: Always verify resulting state (row counts, key cell positions) programmatically after structural changes complete.
